tree.title: What should I do this weekend?
tree.description: A decision tree to help you make the most of your weekend.

branch.content: Solo or with people?
Indoors or outdoors?
High energy or leisurely?
Structured or just physical?
Find a local 5K, amateur sports league, or climbing wall session. Sign up for something that forces you out of the house.
A long run or bike ride with a specific destination rather than a loop. Pick somewhere to end up and figure out the way there.
Nature or urban exploring?
A long walk somewhere genuinely green. Leave your phone in your pocket for at least the first hour. Let your mind wander.
Walk a part of your city you rarely go to. No agenda. Stop whenever something looks interesting. Take your time.
Creative or absorbing?
Something you already do or something new?
Block out the whole morning, put on a long playlist, and make something without a deadline or audience. That is the whole plan.
Pick one thing you have been curious about and spend the day doing the beginner version of it badly. Not learning it. Doing it. That is the difference.
What kind?
Clear the afternoon, have snacks ready, and read a whole book in a day. Turn notifications off. This is harder than it sounds and better than it should be.
Set up properly — comfortable seat, good snacks — and play something you have been meaning to finish without interruption.
Pick a film or series you have been putting off and watch it without checking your phone. Commit to the thing you chose.
Close friends or meeting new people?
How many people?
Low-key or go somewhere?
Cook together or find a market and make up what to do as you go. Those kinds of days tend to figure themselves out.
A gallery, a long walk, a film, a good meal. Pick one thing and commit to it instead of planning six options.
Structured activity or open afternoon?
Book something in advance. Escape room, bowling, mini golf. Takes the planning paralysis away and gives the day a shape.
Someone hosts, someone brings food, someone brings drinks. A long afternoon that turns into an evening without anyone planning for that to happen.
Organised event or more casual?
Find a local class, club, or meetup around something you already care about. Shared interest removes the pressure of small talk.
Say yes to something you would normally decline. The awkwardness is brief. The missed experience is permanent.
